criteria.name: Problem Definition Clarity
criteria.description: The student clearly defines the problem by identifying key elements such as query type, data structure, and performance goals.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student identifies all relevant elements and articulates a clear, precise task.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student misses key elements or provides a vague task definition.

criteria.name: Understanding of Indexing Concepts
criteria.description: The student demonstrates an understanding of indexing concepts as they relate to the problem.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student accurately applies indexing concepts to frame the problem.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student shows confusion about indexing concepts.

criteria.name: Query Optimization Considerations
criteria.description: The student considers query optimization techniques relevant to the problem.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student identifies and incorporates relevant optimization techniques.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student fails to consider any optimization techniques.

typical_errors: Misidentifying the type of query operation involved.
Failing to specify the performance metrics or goals.
Overlooking the role of indexes in query performance.

criteria.name: Error Identification in Index Selection
criteria.description: The student correctly identifies errors or inefficiencies in the AI's index selection.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student identifies all errors and inefficiencies accurately.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student fails to identify major errors or inefficiencies.

criteria.name: Recognition of Query Plan Flaws
criteria.description: The student recognizes flaws or suboptimal choices in the AI-generated query execution plan.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student accurately identifies all flaws in the query execution plan.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student overlooks significant flaws in the execution plan.

criteria.name: Understanding of Execution Cost
criteria.description: The student demonstrates an understanding of the execution cost implications of the AI's output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student accurately assesses the execution cost and its impact on performance.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student misjudges the execution cost or its implications.

typical_errors: Overlooking the impact of missing or incorrect indexes.
Misinterpreting the execution plan's cost metrics.
Failing to recognize unnecessary table scans.

criteria.name: Effective Correction Commands
criteria.description: The student provides clear and effective commands to correct AI errors.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student issues precise commands that effectively correct all identified errors.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student's commands are vague or fail to address the errors.

criteria.name: Appropriate Indexing Recommendations
criteria.description: The student recommends appropriate indexing strategies to improve performance.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student suggests effective indexing strategies that significantly enhance performance.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student suggests inappropriate or ineffective indexing strategies.

criteria.name: Optimization Strategy Adjustment
criteria.description: The student adjusts optimization strategies to steer AI towards better performance.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The student makes well-justified adjustments that improve query performance.
criteria.poor_indicator: The student makes adjustments that do not lead to performance improvements.

typical_errors: Providing correction commands that are too general or irrelevant.
Suggesting indexes that do not align with the query type or data distribution.
Failing to adjust optimization strategies in response to identified issues.
